description: Finish the s3-tests compatibility tail. Two open P2 entries from the prior `ralph/s3-compat-95` cycle remain — closes both and lifts pass rate from 91.5% (162/177) toward ~94%. (1) **Multipart copy GET-side checksum echo divergence.** Three failing s3-tests (`test_multipart_copy_small`, `_special_names`, `_multiple_sizes`): GET on the multipart-copied destination object emits an `x-amz-checksum-*` header that does not match the actual stored bytes — boto3's `FlexibleChecksumError` fires. **Diagnosed root-cause hypothesis** (from code-review of `internal/s3api/multipart.go` + `internal/s3api/checksum.go`): `initiateMultipartUpload` accepts `mu.ChecksumType=""` when client omits `x-amz-checksum-type`. `composeMultipartChecksums` (`checksum.go:206`, `fullObject := false`) then applies COMPOSITE shape (`BASE64(HASH(concat(raws)))-N`) even for CRC64NVME — but AWS spec says CRC64NVME only supports FULL_OBJECT (COMPOSITE+CRC64NVME does not exist on AWS). boto3 1.36+ default-on FlexibleChecksum sends `ChecksumAlgorithm=CRC64NVME` automatically with FULL_OBJECT validation expectation; on GET it streams body + recomputes raw CRC, which never matches our COMPOSITE-with-`-N` shape. The bug is NOT specific to UploadPartCopy — it surfaces on the copy path because boto3's high-level transfer manager triggers auto-checksum default-on, while native multipart tests in `US-003` of the prior cycle set algorithm/type explicitly and avoided the gap. (2) **Multipart Complete rejects duplicate PartNumber in Parts list.** One failing s3-test (`test_multipart_resend_first_finishes_last`): `multipart.go:430` enforces `if p.PartNumber <= prev` and returns `InvalidPartOrder`. AWS / RGW dedupe duplicates by taking the latest storage-side write; relax to `< prev` + dedupe to LAST ETag per PartNumber. 4 stories. Closes ROADMAP P2 'Multipart copy GET-side checksum echo divergence' + P2 'Multipart Complete rejects duplicate PartNumber in Parts list' on cycle close.

userStories.title: ChecksumType defaults for CRC family on multipart Initiate
userStories.description: As an S3 client using boto3 1.36+ FlexibleChecksum default-on, I want CRC64NVME multipart uploads (with no explicit `x-amz-checksum-type` header) to be persisted as FULL_OBJECT so GET-side `x-amz-checksum-crc64nvme` validates against the destination body bytes — matching AWS behaviour and unblocking three failing s3-tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a new in-process Go test in `internal/s3api/multipart_copy_test.go` (or a fresh `multipart_checksum_default_test.go`) that drives the failing scenario: Initiate multipart upload with `x-amz-checksum-algorithm: CRC64NVME` and NO `x-amz-checksum-type` header → UploadPartCopy from a populated source → Complete → GET destination with `x-amz-checksum-mode: ENABLED`. Test asserts the emitted `x-amz-checksum-crc64nvme` header equals a fresh raw CRC64NVME over the destination body bytes (FULL_OBJECT shape, NO `-N` suffix). Test is initially RED, locking the bug.

`internal/s3api/multipart.go::initiateMultipartUpload`: after building `mu`, if `mu.ChecksumAlgorithm == "CRC64NVME"` and `mu.ChecksumType == ""` → force `mu.ChecksumType = "FULL_OBJECT"` (AWS spec: CRC64NVME only supports FULL_OBJECT)
Reject explicitly: `mu.ChecksumAlgorithm == "CRC64NVME" && mu.ChecksumType == "COMPOSITE"` → 400 InvalidArgument with the AWS-parity error message
For CRC32 / CRC32C with empty `mu.ChecksumType` → default to `FULL_OBJECT` (modern boto3 default; AWS accepts both, FULL_OBJECT lets the body-stream-recompute pathway succeed)
SHA1 / SHA256 with empty type stay COMPOSITE (AWS rejects SHA + FULL_OBJECT — keep current behaviour)
The new in-process test flips RED → GREEN with the fix

Existing native-multipart tests (`internal/s3api/checksum_test.go`, `multipart_test.go`, `multipart_checksum_test.go`, `multipart_complete_checksum_test.go`, `multipart_copy_test.go`) still pass — explicit-algorithm + explicit-type uploads unchanged; only the empty-type defaulting behaviour changes

Run `make up-all` + `scripts/s3-tests/run.sh` filtered to `test_multipart_copy_small`, `test_multipart_copy_special_names`, `test_multipart_copy_multiple_sizes` against the local stack; record the new pass / fail status in `progress.txt`

If any of the three s3-tests still fail after the fix lands, append a `## US-001 Followup` section to `progress.txt` with the secondary mismatch (expected vs got) and either extend US-001 with a follow-up commit or carve a new story BEFORE proceeding to US-002
Update `scripts/s3-tests/README.md` baseline section: lift pass count by +3 (or by the number actually flipped, whichever is true)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Initiate-time defaulting in `internal/s3api/multipart.go::applyChecksumTypeDefault`: CRC64NVME / CRC32 / CRC32C empty-type → FULL_OBJECT; CRC64NVME + COMPOSITE → 400 InvalidArgument. New test file `multipart_checksum_default_test.go` (3 cases) is RED before the fix lands and GREEN after. Existing tests that leaned on empty-type=COMPOSITE for CRC32 / CRC32C updated to send explicit COMPOSITE header to preserve intent. Targeted s3-tests run deferred to US-003 — Lima Docker daemon not running on this host. `go vet ./...` clean. `go test ./...` green.

userStories.title: Multipart Complete dedupes duplicate PartNumber in Parts list
userStories.description: As an S3 client retrying multipart upload mid-stream, I want CompleteMultipartUpload to accept a Parts list that names the same PartNumber twice (latest ETag wins) — matching AWS / RGW behaviour — instead of rejecting with `InvalidPartOrder`.
acceptanceCriteria: `internal/s3api/multipart.go::completeMultipartUpload` strict-ascending check at line ~430 relaxed from `if p.PartNumber <= prev` to `if p.PartNumber < prev` (equal allowed)
Per-PartNumber dedupe before the per-part ETag walk: keep the LAST entry for each unique PartNumber (preserves submission order; matches AWS take-latest semantics)

Per-part ETag validation runs against the LAST entry's ETag for that PartNumber, compared against the LATEST stored part (existing `meta.Store.SavePart` overwrite-on-resave gives last-write-wins on the storage side; verify on all 3 backends via storetest contract)
S3-test passes: `test_multipart_resend_first_finishes_last`

New unit test in `internal/s3api/multipart_partnumber_test.go`: submitting `[{1, etagA}, {1, etagB}]` accepts and validates against the LATEST stored PartNumber=1 (which is etagB after the resend); `[1, 3, 2]` still returns `InvalidPartOrder`
Update `scripts/s3-tests/README.md` baseline section: lift pass count by +1
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: S3-tests baseline rerun + ROADMAP close-flip
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a recorded post-cycle baseline so the lifted s3-tests pass count is committed and the two ROADMAP P2 entries flip to Done in the same commit.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `scripts/s3-tests/run.sh` against the full local stack (`make up-all`); capture the new pass count + diff vs prior baseline (162/177 → ~166/177)
Update `scripts/s3-tests/README.md` baseline section with the new headline number + per-test diff
ROADMAP.md close-flip in the same commit per CLAUDE.md Roadmap maintenance rule: both P2 entries flip to `~~**P2 — ...**~~ — **Done.** ... (commit `<pending>`)`

If any of the 4 target s3-tests still fail after US-001/US-002, file the failing axis as a new ROADMAP entry BEFORE flipping the headline (per CLAUDE.md 'Discovering a new gap' rule); do NOT flip if any are open
Closing-SHA backfill follow-up commit on main per established pattern (commit-then-amend is undesirable)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
